repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal states this recanting-witness response functional; the prior scm_prox_recant_pid angle concerned a simple natural-direct-effect proxy program.

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 reduces declared finite discrete SCM restrictions and causal targets to sharp polynomial programs; this is a model-specific instantiation.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry states the exact containment for this nested response functional.

published_prior_art.cite: GabrielSachsSjolander2023 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 provide the no-proxy mediation-bound baseline; marginalizing a factorized completion is routine supporting machinery.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry states this exact reduction.

published_prior_art.cite: This is the elementary no-information limit of a finite response-function construction.

repo_prior_art.cite: The earlier scm_prox_recant_pid natural-direct-effect angle did not ask the nested recanting-witness endpoint-exclusion question.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified source characterizes endpoint exclusion for this fixed-K proxy-factorized nested target; closest results are BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026 under bridge-based identification, DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 for natural effects, and GabrielSachsSjolander2023 without proxies.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits the closer proximal-mediation results DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 and the public recanting-witness preprint WuBaiCui2026 (arXiv:2606.17600), plus ZhangShanLi2026 (arXiv:2607.02901); none is shown to subsume this target, but all must be positioned.

soundness_flags.label: ass:randomization
soundness_flags.one_line: Randomized assignment is standard, but BaiWuSunCui2026 studies proximal mediation with unmeasured confounding and is not an appropriate supporting citation for the stated independence condition; replace the citation.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:strict-proxy-contraction / ass:w-rank-deficiency
soundness_flags.one_line: For the headline K=3 binary submodel, the outcome-proxy map has at most two rows, so rank{C_W}<3 is automatic and does not encode an informative failed-completeness regime; require rank two plus a quantitative nondegeneracy/separation condition or revise the headline.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Finite proxy-factorized sensitivity model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Fixed K, the positive latent-class mass floor, and conditionally independent proxy measurements are accepted as a conditional finite latent-measurement regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any strict endpoint-exclusion result is only for the stated finite proxy-factorized model, not unrestricted latent cardinality, general proximal models, point identification, or inference.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add and distinguish DukesShpitserTchetgen2023, WuBaiCui2026, and ZhangShanLi2026; state their estimands, proxy assumptions, and why none supplies this factorization-constrained endpoint certificate.

Replace the automatic binary-K=3 rank inequality with rank(C_W)=2 and an explicit minor/separation condition, then show that the condition leaves a genuinely incomplete but informative measurement regime.
Use the endpoint-elimination handle to construct a rational strictly positive K=3 witness and prove that endpoint exclusion persists on an open semialgebraic neighborhood of observed tables.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature positioning and repair the vacuous headline rank condition, then pursue a positive robust strict-contraction witness as the field-level kernel.
